text.title: k8s-resource-quota Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for k8s-resource-quota as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Setting identical ResourceQuota for all namespaces regardless of team size or workload type.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: One-size-fits-all quotas either over-constrain small teams or under-constrain large ones. Quota should be proportional to team need with 15-20% headroom reserved for burst.

failure-mode.description: Setting quota limits.memory without matching requests.memory.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pods using LimitRange defaults contribute to limits quota but not requests quota. The scheduler uses requests, not limits. Tracking only one plane creates an accounting mismatch that allows more pods than intended to be scheduled.

failure-mode.description: Never tuning quotas after initial setup.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Team size and workload patterns change. An untouched quota becomes either a permanent bottleneck (too tight) or a meaningless rubber stamp (too generous). Review quota utilization monthly and tune based on kube_resourcequota metrics.
